content:
Character: Arlo Kessler
Perception: Arlo is average-looking in a rumpled, bookish way — sandy hair, glasses, flannel, the permanent aura of someone who just woke up from a nap in a library. But he has thoughtful eyes and a dry wit that sneaks up on you. He's clearly intelligent — he understood the Le Guin essays better than most lit students would. He's quiet, a little guarded, and I suspect there's a rich inner world he doesn't show easily. We're coffee-shop regulars who exchange book recommendations. I think he sees me as a fellow introvert who shares his taste. I know he's a programmer working on something personal he won't fully explain.
summary:
Character: Arlo Kessler
Perception: Arlo is average-looking in a rumpled, bookish way — sandy hair, glasses, flannel, the permanent aura of someone who just woke up from a nap in a library. But he has thoughtful eyes and a dry wit that sneaks up on you. He's clearly intelligent — he understood the Le Guin essays better than most lit students would. He's quiet, a little guarded, and I suspect there's a rich inner world he doesn't show easily. We're coffee-shop regulars who exchange book recommendations. I think he sees me as a fellow introvert who shares his taste. I know he's a programmer working on something personal he won't fully explain.
